oligos.name: Indexed bead seqA
oligos.role: Bead capture oligo (design A) - split-pool cell barcode + UMI + poly(dT)
oligos.notes: Combinatorial cell barcode built by 3 split-pool rounds (96^3 possible barcodes). ~1e7-1e8 oligos per bead, all sharing one cell barcode but different UMIs.

oligos.name: Indexed bead seqB
oligos.role: Bead capture oligo (design B) - one base shorter spacer than seqA

components.name: spacer (seqB)
components.role: phasing spacer (2 nt vs 4 nt in seqA)

oligos.notes: seqB uses Read1 primer Read1 SeqB-TAC; differs from seqA only by the ISPCR-spacer length.

oligos.name: Split-pool round 1 oligo (A-1 .. A-96)
oligos.role: First-round amino-modified barcode oligo conjugated to bead (introduces barcode A + linker 1)
oligos.notes: 96 variants (Table S1, A-1..A-96); 5'-amino-modified, EDC-coupled to carboxyl beads. Example A-1 barcode = TTTAGG, A-2 = ATTCCA, A-3 = GCTCAA.

oligos.name: Split-pool round 2 oligo (B-1 .. B-96)
oligos.role: Second-round oligo (rev-complementary to linker 1, adds barcode B + linker 2) introduced by single-cycle PCR
oligos.notes: 96 variants (Table S1, B-1..B-96). CGATCGTGTCACCGA = rev-comp of linker 2; CCCTGTAGTGAGTCG = rev-comp of linker 1. Example B-1 barcode = CCTAAA.

oligos.name: Split-pool round 3 oligo (C-1 .. C-96)
oligos.role: Third-round oligo introducing poly(dT), UMI and barcode C
oligos.notes: 96 variants (Table S1, C-1..C-96). Poly(A)30 templates the bead poly(dT); NNNNNN = UMI; 6 nt barcode C; CGATCGTGTCACCGA anneals to linker 2. Example C-1 barcode = CCTAAA.

oligos.name: TSO LNA
oligos.notes: Printed as AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAAT/rG/rG/iXNA_G; terminal GGG are two riboguanosines + one locked/xeno (LNA) guanosine for efficient template switching (Smart-seq2).

oligos.name: TSO-PCR (ISPCR)
oligos.notes: Anneals to the ISPCR handle present at both ends of the RT/template-switch product.

oligos.name: P5
oligos.role: Selective-PCR / P5 library primer (replaces Nextera index-2/i5 primer)
oligos.notes: Printed AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACGCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT-s-A-s-C; last two bonds phosphorothioate. Used with a Nextera N7xx/N8xx i7 primer for the final 3'-end-enrichment PCR.

oligos.name: Read1 SeqA-GT
oligos.notes: Reads directly into cell barcode round 1 on seqA libraries.

oligos.name: Read1 SeqB-TAC
oligos.notes: seqB variant, 2 nt shorter 3' end than SeqA-GT.

oligos.name: Nextera i7 index primer (N701-N712, N801-N824)
oligos.role: i7 sample-index PCR primer (Tn5 side of selective PCR)

components.name: Nextera i7 handle
components.role: anneals to Tn5 mosaic-end read handle

oligos.notes: 8 nt indices: N701 TAAGGCGA, N702 CGTACTAG, N703 AGGCAGAA, N704 TCCTGAGC, N705 GGACTCCT, N706 TAGGCATG, N707 CTCTCTAC, N708 CAGAGAGG, N709 GCTACGCT, N710 CGAGGCTG, N711 AAGAGGCA, N712 GTAGAGGA; N801-N810 repeat N701-N710, N811 GCTCATGA, N812 ATCTCAGG, N813 ACTCGCTA, N814 GGAGCTAC, N815 GCGTAGTA, N816 CGGAGCCT, N817 TACGCTGC, N818 ATGCGCAG, N819 TAGCGCTC, N820 ACTGAGCG, N821 CCTAAGAC, N822 CGATCAGT, N823 TGCAGCTA, N824 TCGACGTC.

final_library.source_label: Microwell-seq (Han et al., Cell 2018) - final Illumina 3' library
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC(P5) GCCTGTCCGCGG(linker) AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT(ISPCR) ACGT [CELL_BARCODE:6](BC1) CGACTCACTACAGGG(lnk1) [CELL_BARCODE:6](BC2) TCGGTGACACGATCG(lnk2) [CELL_BARCODE:6](BC3) [UMI:6] TTTT...(30, poly-dT) [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT(ME) CCGAGCCCACGAGAC(i7 handle) [SAMPLE_INDEX:8](i7) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG(P7)
annotation_lines: GCCTGTCCGCGG = custom linker (added by P5 primer, Read1 primer binding site)
AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT = ISPCR / Smart-seq2 handle (from bead & TSO)

evidence.locator: Microwell-seq (Han et al., Cell 2018) - final Illumina 3' library

library_generation.title: mRNA capture on barcoded beads
library_generation.summary: Single cells and barcoded magnetic beads are co-trapped in agarose microwells and lysed so poly(A) mRNA hybridizes to the bead poly(dT).
library_generation.note: 10^5 microwells trap 5-10 K cells; each bead carries 10^7-10^8 copies of one cell barcode. After 12 min lysis, beads are retrieved with a magnet.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription + template switching
library_generation.summary: SuperScript II extends the bead poly(dT) to make first-strand cDNA, and the TSO LNA adds the ISPCR handle to the cDNA 3' end (Smart-seq2).
library_generation.note: RT mix with betaine/MgCl2 and 1 uM TSO; both ends of the product now carry the AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT handle.

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I treatment
library_generation.summary: Exonuclease I digests bead oligos that failed to capture/extend an mRNA.
library_generation.note: 37C, 60 min; removes single-stranded unextended capture oligos before amplification.

library_generation.title: cDNA amplification (single-primer ISPCR)
library_generation.summary: KAPA HiFi PCR with the single TSO-PCR primer amplifies full-length barcoded cDNA off both ISPCR handles.
library_generation.note: 4 cycles 65C anneal then 10-14 cycles 67C; product purified with AMPure XP.

library_generation.title: Tn5 tagmentation
library_generation.summary: A customized transposase carrying two identical mosaic-end insertion sequences fragments the amplified cDNA and appends adapters at both ends.
library_generation.note: TruePrep/Vazyme Tn5 (both ends identical), so productive fragments must be rescued by asymmetric selective PCR.

library_generation.title: Selective PCR (P5 + i7)
library_generation.summary: P5 anneals to the ISPCR/bead handle and a Nextera N7xx i7 primer anneals to the Tn5 handle, enriching only the transcript 3' end and adding flow-cell adapters + sample index.
library_generation.note: Index-2/i5 primer of the kit is replaced by P5; non-3' fragments self-loop and are not amplified. 14 cycles; AMPure XP cleanup; ~400-700 bp product on the Illumina HiSeq.

library_sequencing.primer: Read1 SeqA-GT (GCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACGT) / Read1 SeqB-TAC for seqB beads
library_sequencing.note: Custom Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand across the custom-linker + ISPCR handle and reads the 3-part split-pool cell barcode (3 x 6 nt) interleaved with linker 1 (15 nt) and linker 2 (15 nt), then the 6 nt UMI, then poly(dT). Drop-seq preprocessing rejects reads missing the two linkers / poly-T. seqB primer is 2 nt shorter at the 3' end (…AGAGTAC vs …AGAGTACGT).
library_sequencing.diagram:
top   5'-GCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACGT NNNNNN CGACTCACTACAGGG NNNNNN TCGGTGACACGATCG NNNNNN NNNNNN TTTTT…-3'
         ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||| |BC1 | ||||||||||||||| |BC2 | ||||||||||||||| |BC3 | | UMI| |||||
bot   3'-CGGACAGGCGCCTTCGTCACCATAGTTGCGTCTCATGCA NNNNNN GCTGAGTGATGTCCC NNNNNN AGCCACTGTGCTAGC NNNNNN NNNNNN AAAAA…-5'
primer   5'-GCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACGT----------------------------------------------------------------->
            (Read1 SeqA-GT anneals to bottom strand; reads BC1-lnk1-BC2-lnk2-BC3-UMI, 54 nt)

library_sequencing.primer: Standard Nextera i7 index-read primer (5'-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC-3', ME + i7 read handle)
library_sequencing.note: Primer anneals to the bottom strand across the Tn5 mosaic-end + Nextera i7 read handle and reads the 8 nt sample index contributed by the N701–N712 / N801–N824 primer (read as the reverse complement of the designed index). No i5 index — the i5 position is replaced by the custom P5 primer.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top   5'-…[cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC NNNNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
              ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||   i7    ||||||||||||||||||||||||
bot   3'-…[cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG NNNNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
primer   5'-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC-------->
            (anneals to bottom strand; reads the 8 nt i7 index)

library_sequencing.note: Nextera Read 2 primer anneals to the top strand across the i7 read handle + mosaic end and reads into the fragmented 3' cDNA insert from the Tn5 side. Read length is set by the sequencing run (run-dependent, not fixed by the design), so no fixed cycle count.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top   5'-…[cDNA insert]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC NNNNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
                      ||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||   i7    ||||||||||||||||||||||||
bot   3'-…[cDNA insert]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG NNNNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
primer            <-------GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5' (Nextera Read2 primer, anneals to top strand, reads cDNA)

title: Microwell-seq
description: Microwell-seq is a high-throughput, low-cost droplet-free scRNA-seq platform in which single cells are trapped in an agarose microwell array (~100,000 wells) alongside barcoded magnetic beads. Each bead carries poly(dT) capture oligos bearing a 3-part split-pool cell barcode (3 x 6 nt) and a 6 nt UMI; captured mRNA is reverse-transcribed with Smart-seq2 template switching, amplified by single-primer ISPCR, tagmented by a customized Tn5 carrying two identical mosaic ends, and 3'-enriched by selective PCR (P5 + Nextera i7). Read 1 reads the split cell barcode + UMI, Read 2 reads the cDNA fragment, and the i7 index read reads the sample index on an Illumina HiSeq.

original_publication.title: Mapping the Mouse Cell Atlas by Microwell-Seq

throughput.summary: >400,000 single cells profiled across >50 mouse tissues/cultures; >800 cell types in 98 major clusters; library cost <0.02 USD per cell; <1.2% cell doublets.
throughput.cells: >400,000 single cells (5-10 K per microwell array run)
throughput.rna: ~6,500 genes and ~55,000 transcripts detected per cell at saturated sequencing

publication.statistical_model: UMI-based digital gene expression (molecular counting via Drop-seq pipeline); dimensionality reduction/clustering with Seurat (t-SNE), and cell-type assignment by Pearson/Spearman correlation to reference transcriptomes (scMCA).

other.label: Microwell array
other.value: 5% agarose cast from PDMS; ~100,000 microwells, 28 um diameter x 35 um depth

other.label: Bead
other.value: Carboxyl magnetic beads, 20-25 um (Suzhou Knowledge & Benefit Sphere Tech)

other.label: Tagmentation
other.value: Customized Tn5 with two identical mosaic ends (TruePrep DNA Library Prep Kit V2, Vazyme); index-2/i5 primer replaced by custom P5

data_processing.summary: Reads were processed with the Drop-seq core computational pipeline: reads lacking the two bead linkers or poly-T are discarded, the cell barcode + UMI are parsed from Read 1, the cDNA (Read 2) is aligned to the genome with STAR, UMIs are collapsed per gene into a digital gene-expression (DGE) matrix, and cells are then clustered in Seurat and typed against the scMCA reference by expression correlation.
data_processing.statistical_model: UMI-based molecular counting (digital gene expression); cell-type calls by Pearson/Spearman correlation to reference transcriptomes — no parametric count distribution specified
